persona_description: Wallingford strongly emphasizes educational excellence and robust social services, reflecting a commitment to its residents' well-being. The town also demonstrates fiscal prudence through careful budget management and a focus on maintaining a strong financial standing.
key_facts: The Fiscal Year 2025-2026 budget was adopted on May 13, 2025.
The mill rate for FY2025-26 is 24.12 mills, a decrease of 5.82 mills from the previous year's 30.66 mills due to revaluation.
The average residential property parcel is assessed at $277,618, which would generate $6,896 in taxes.
The Board of Education budget reflects an 8% increase in employee health insurance, reduced from an initially estimated 10%.
The town maintains a strong unassigned fund balance of $22 million as of the 2023-24 Audit.
strengths: Commitment to maintaining an Aa1 credit rating, supported by a substantial $22 million unassigned fund balance.
Proactive in modernizing government with the implementation of a new consumer-friendly online bill-pay system expected to be operational by the next tax payment due date.
Focus on retaining town employees through competitive and rewarding employment, including negotiated wage increases in collective bargaining agreements.
weaknesses: Significant increases in operating costs are driven by employee health insurance (8%), debt service (up $137,967), and actuarial pension funding recommendations.
Prior budget leveling funds (CRRA and ARPA funds) have been exhausted, limiting tools to absorb capital expenditures.
Downward trends in revenue from sales are noted in both the Water and Sewer Divisions.
